outcome_contract.why: Cleaning staff have no way to signal when a room is done. Reception staff checking guests in are flying blind on whether the room is actually ready. Fixing this means both teams can do their jobs without phone calls or guesswork.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Cleaning staff can mark individual rooms as cleaned on the prepare dashboard. The checkins screen shows a per-room cleanliness indicator. Reception staff can see at a glance whether a room is ready before a guest reaches the desk.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Mark Clean button per row in `CleaningPriorityTable`; cleanliness pill in `view/BookingRow` 8th cell; "Room Ready" header in `TableHeader`
Button disabled when `!isToday`; button disabled during write; error toast on failure; already-clean state visible in pill
N/A — same Firebase auth as all reception staff; no PII in room status
`try/catch` + `showToast("Failed to mark room as clean", "error")` in `PrepareDashboard` handler
Guard unit test; button interaction test + mock update; cleanliness pill test; prop-threading test; TableHeader snapshot update
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: 5 test files; TableHeader test reconciles pre-existing Email Booking mismatch

engineering_coverage.applicable: `SingleRoomStatus` schema unchanged; `usePrepareDashboardData()` return additive; guard: `clean === "Yes" && typeof cleaned === "string" && toEpochMillis(cleaned) >= todayTime`
Single `useRoomStatusData()` at `CheckinsTable` level; prop-thread through `CheckinsTableView → BookingRow`; no per-row listeners
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: `useRoomStatusMutations()` has no subscription overhead; calling it in `PrepareDashboard` (TASK-03) does not create a duplicate listener — `usePrepareDashboardData()` only calls it for internal write path; the "single subscription" constraint applies to `useRoomStatusData()` only

engineering_coverage.applicable: Cloudflare Worker hot-swap; no DB migration; Firebase writes idempotent; no feature flag needed

stage_payload.summary: Connects the prepare dashboard and checkins screen via the existing `/roomStatus` Firebase path. Cleaning staff get a per-room "Mark Clean" button (today-only) on the prepare dashboard. Reception staff get a per-row cleanliness pill on the checkins table. The auto-sync guard bug is patched first so manual clean signals from occupied rooms are never silently overwritten. All changes are additive; no new Firebase paths or npm dependencies are required.
goals: Cleaning staff can mark a room as cleaned on the prepare dashboard (today-only)
Manual clean signals are never overwritten by the auto-sync for occupied rooms
Reception staff see a per-room cleanliness pill on the checkins screen
No new Firebase paths or npm dependencies
constraints: `SingleRoomStatus` Zod schema must remain backward compatible
`/roomStatus/index_<room>` is the write target — no new paths
UI must use reception design system tokens (Tailwind v4 semantic tokens)
Tests run in CI only — validate by pushing to dev branch (testing policy)
assumptions: `roomAllocated` in `CheckInRow` holds raw room number (e.g. "3") → Firebase key `index_3`
`useFirebaseSubscription` creates one `onValue` listener per hook instance — no React-layer deduplication
`usePrepareDashboardData()` currently returns an inferred object (no explicit interface); adding `roomStatusMap` is additive and does not break callers
selected_approach: What was chosen: Full pipeline — Mark Clean button on prepare dashboard + auto-sync guard patch + per-room cleanliness pill on checkins screen

Why planning is not reopening option selection: Analysis resolved all sub-architecture choices (subscription placement, prepare-side wiring, today-only gating, await-not-optimistic UX) and closed all open questions with defaults

task_briefs.title: Mark Clean button on prepare dashboard

areas.label: Cleaning staff workflow
areas.trigger: Cleaning staff finishes cleaning a room
areas.flow: 1. Staff opens `/prepare-dashboard` for today; 2. Each room row shows "Mark Clean" button (disabled for past/future dates); 3. Staff clicks button → button disables during write → Firebase `/roomStatus/index_<room>` updated with `{ clean: "Yes", cleaned: <ISO timestamp> }` → chip refreshes to "Clean" in real-time; 4. If write fails: toast error shown, chip stays "Dirty"
areas.seams: Error path: network failure shows toast; room stays "Dirty"

areas.label: Auto-sync (stayover guard)
areas.flow: For each room: if `clean === "Yes"` AND `typeof cleaned === "string"` AND `toEpochMillis(cleaned) >= todayTime` → skip dirty overwrite; stayover clean survives until midnight
areas.seams: Occupants who check in after manual clean still show as Dirty on next auto-sync cycle — this is acceptable (manual re-clean may be needed)

areas.label: Reception checkins view
areas.flow: 1. Reception opens `/checkin`; 2. Per-row "Room Ready" pill visible in 8th column for each booking row; 3. Green "Clean" or red "Dirty" derived from `/roomStatus/index_<roomAllocated>`; 4. Single Firebase subscription at `CheckinsTable` level; all rows read from shared `roomStatusMap` prop
areas.seams: `roomAllocated` undefined → pill renders nothing (graceful); `colSpan` update required alongside header
